revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sakurakyo Kofun]][[en:Sakurakyō Kofun]][[ja:桜京古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sakurakyo Kofun]][[en:Sakurakyō Kofun]][[ja:桜京古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sakurakyo Kofun]][[en:Sakurakyō Kofun]][[ja:桜京古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q48760086]] Wikidata name: Sakurakyo Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Munakata, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Sakurakyō Kofun | native_name =桜京古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sakurakyo Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sakurakyō Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sakurakyō Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.843797|N|130.495467|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Munakata, Fukuoka|en|Munakata, Fukuoka|WD=Q819603|lt=Munakata, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|50|38|N|130|29|43|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sakurakyō Kofun'''|桜京古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Mutajiri neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Munakata, Fukuoka|en|Munakata, Fukuoka|WD=Q819603|lt=Munakata}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/139747|title=桜京古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sakurakyō Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is the center of a cluster of approximately 80 Kofuns facing the [[Genkai Sea]] on a hill with an elevation of about 42 meters. It has a total length is about 41 meters; the diameter of the posterior circular portion is about 18 meters, the height is about 4 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion is about 10 meters with a height of 2.5 meters. The tumulus is orientated to the south, and the west side is slightly deformed. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' or shards of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' have been found, nor has any trace of a moat been discovered. The horizontal-entry cave-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a multi-chambered structure in the center of the posterior circle, opening to the west. The back room is 3.7 meters long, about 2.2 meters wide, and about 3.6 meters high, with a stone house shaped {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus}} attached to the rear wall, and the front room is about half the size of the back room. It was already opened in 1673, and no {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} have been passed down. The tumulus is a [[decorated kofun]]. The pillar stones and back wall are divided into sections with a triangular pattern made of vertical, horizontal, and diagonal line carvings, and each section is painted with red, yellow, and blue pigments, and the upper part is painted red. Judging from the structure of the burial chamber and the shape of the tumulus, it is believed to have been constructed in the 6th century, or the last Kofun period. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> From the five small {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular tumuli surrounding the main tumulus, iron arrowheads, metal utensils (bow fittings), and shards of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} pottery have been found.
Although many decorated ''kofun'' have been found along the coast of the {{ill|Ariake Sea|en|Ariake Sea|WD=Q49557|lt=Ariake Sea}} in the Kyushu, this is the only one to have been found in along the coast of the Genkai Sea, and its presence outside the distribution area for decorative ''kofun'' suggests cultural or political interaction between the regions. This ''kofun'' was included in the {{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|WD=Q7809|lt=UNESCO}} {{ill|World Heritage Site|en|World Heritage Site|WD=Q9259|lt=World Heritage Site}} {{ill|Sacred Island of Okinoshima and Associated Sites in the Munakata Region|en|Sacred Island of Okinoshima and Associated Sites in the Munakata Region|WD=Q324638|lt=Sacred Island of Okinoshima and Associated Sites in the Munakata Region}} submission in 2009, but was later removed from the constituent assets when it was officially registered in 2017.<ref name="Okinoshima">{{cite web |url=https://whc.unesco.org/en/tentativelists/5400/ |title=Okinoshima Island and Related Sites in Munakata Region |publisher={{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|WD=Q7809|lt=UNESCO}} |access-date=7 December 2013}}</ref><ref name="Okinoshima2">{{cite web |url=http://www.okinoshima-heritage.jp/pamphlets/img/en/pdf/OkinoshimaIsland.pdf |title=Okinoshima Island and Related Sites in Munakata Region |publisher=World Heritage Promotion Committee of "Okinoshima Island and Related Sites in Munakata Region" |pages=6f |access-date=7 December 2013 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131212072806/http://www.okinoshima-heritage.jp/pamphlets/img/en/pdf/OkinoshimaIsland.pdf |archive-date=12 December 2013 }}</ref>
The tumulus is approximately 2.4 kilometers northwest of [[:en:Munakata Shrine|Munakata Shrine]] or 7.4 kilometers (15 minutes by car]] from {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|en|Kagoshima Main Line|WD=Q1187520|lt=Kagoshima Main Line}} {{ill|Tōgō Station|en|Tōgō Station|WD=Q5359652|lt=Tōgō Station}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|WD=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}} *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.munakata.lg.jp/w018/030/020/020/500/201501270052.html Munakata City home page] {{in lang|ja}} Sakurakyō Kofun

This ''kofun'' was included in the {{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|WD=Q7809|lt=UNESCO}} {{ill|World Heritage Site|en|World Heritage Site|WD=Q9259|lt=World Heritage Site}} {{ill|Sacred Island of Okinoshima and Associated Sites in the Munakata Region|en|Sacred Island of Okinoshima and Associated Sites in the Munakata Region|WD=Q324638|lt=Sacred Island of Okinoshima and Associated Sites in the Munakata Region}} submission in 2009, but was later removed from the constituent assets when it was officially registered in 2017.<ref name="Okinoshima">{{cite web |url=https://whc.unesco.org/en/tentativelists/5400/ |title=Okinoshima Island and Related Sites in Munakata Region |publisher={{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|WD=Q7809|lt=UNESCO}} |access-date=7 December 2013}}</ref><ref name="Okinoshima2">{{cite web |url=http://www.okinoshima-heritage.jp/pamphlets/img/en/pdf/OkinoshimaIsland.pdf |title=Okinoshima Island and Related Sites in Munakata Region |publisher=World Heritage Promotion Committee of "Okinoshima Island and Related Sites in Munakata Region" |pages=6f |access-date=7 December 2013 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131212072806/http://www.okinoshima-heritage.jp/pamphlets/img/en/pdf/OkinoshimaIsland.pdf |archive-date=12 December 2013 }}</ref>
The tumulus is approximately 2.4 kilometers northwest of [[:en:Munakata Shrine|Munakata Shrine]] or 7.4 kilometers (15 minutes by car]] from {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|en|Kagoshima Main Line|WD=Q1187520|lt=Kagoshima Main Line}} {{ill|Tōgō Station|en|Tōgō Station|WD=Q5359652|lt=Tōgō Station}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|WD=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}} *[[Decorated kofun]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sakurakyo Kofun]][[en:Sakurakyō Kofun]][[ja:桜京古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sakurakyo Kofun]][[en:Sakurakyō Kofun]][[ja:桜京古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sakurakyo Kofun]][[en:Sakurakyō Kofun]][[ja:桜京古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q48760086]] Wikidata name: Sakurakyo Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Munakata, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Sakurakyō Kofun | native_name =桜京古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sakurakyo Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sakurakyō Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sakurakyō Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.843797|N|130.495467|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Munakata, Fukuoka|en|Munakata, Fukuoka|WD=Q819603|lt=Munakata, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|50|38|N|130|29|43|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sakurakyō Kofun'''|桜京古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Mutajiri neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Munakata, Fukuoka|en|Munakata, Fukuoka|WD=Q819603|lt=Munakata}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/139747|title=桜京古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sakurakyō Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is the center of a cluster of approximately 80 Kofuns facing the [[Genkai Sea]] on a hill with an elevation of about 42 meters. It has a total length is about 41 meters; the diameter of the posterior circular portion is about 18 meters, the height is about 4 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion is about 10 meters with a height of 2.5 meters. The tumulus is orientated to the south, and the west side is slightly deformed. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' or shards of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' have been found, nor has any trace of a moat been discovered. The horizontal-entry cave-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a multi-chambered structure in the center of the posterior circle, opening to the west. The back room is 3.7 meters long, about 2.2 meters wide, and about 3.6 meters high, with a stone house shaped {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus}} attached to the rear wall, and the front room is about half the size of the back room. It was already opened in 1673, and no {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} have been passed down. The tumulus is a [[decorated kofun]]. The pillar stones and back wall are divided into sections with a triangular pattern made of vertical, horizontal, and diagonal line carvings, and each section is painted with red, yellow, and blue pigments, and the upper part is painted red. Judging from the structure of the burial chamber and the shape of the tumulus, it is believed to have been constructed in the 6th century, or the last Kofun period. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> From the five small {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular tumuli surrounding the main tumulus, iron arrowheads, metal utensils (bow fittings), and shards of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} pottery have been found.
Although many decorated ''kofun'' have been found along the coast of the {{ill|Ariake Sea|en|Ariake Sea|WD=Q49557|lt=Ariake Sea}} in the Kyushu, this is the only one to have been found in along the coast of the Genkai Sea, and its presence outside the distribution area for decorative ''kofun'' suggests cultural or political interaction between the regions. This ''kofun'' was included in the {{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|WD=Q7809|lt=UNESCO}} {{ill|World Heritage Site|en|World Heritage Site|WD=Q9259|lt=World Heritage Site}} {{ill|Sacred Island of Okinoshima and Associated Sites in the Munakata Region|en|Sacred Island of Okinoshima and Associated Sites in the Munakata Region|WD=Q324638|lt=Sacred Island of Okinoshima and Associated Sites in the Munakata Region}} submission in 2009, but was later removed from the constituent assets when it was officially registered in 2017.<ref name="Okinoshima">{{cite web |url=https://whc.unesco.org/en/tentativelists/5400/ |title=Okinoshima Island and Related Sites in Munakata Region |publisher={{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|WD=Q7809|lt=UNESCO}} |access-date=7 December 2013}}</ref><ref name="Okinoshima2">{{cite web |url=http://www.okinoshima-heritage.jp/pamphlets/img/en/pdf/OkinoshimaIsland.pdf |title=Okinoshima Island and Related Sites in Munakata Region |publisher=World Heritage Promotion Committee of "Okinoshima Island and Related Sites in Munakata Region" |pages=6f |access-date=7 December 2013 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131212072806/http://www.okinoshima-heritage.jp/pamphlets/img/en/pdf/OkinoshimaIsland.pdf |archive-date=12 December 2013 }}</ref>
The tumulus is approximately 2.4 kilometers northwest of [[:en:Munakata Shrine|Munakata Shrine]] or 7.4 kilometers (15 minutes by car]] from {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|en|Kagoshima Main Line|WD=Q1187520|lt=Kagoshima Main Line}} {{ill|Tōgō Station|en|Tōgō Station|WD=Q5359652|lt=Tōgō Station}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|WD=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}} *[[Decorated kofun]]

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/139747|title=桜京古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sakurakyō Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is the center of a cluster of approximately 80 Kofuns facing the [[Genkai Sea]] on a hill with an elevation of about 42 meters. It has a total length is about 41 meters; the diameter of the posterior circular portion is about 18 meters, the height is about 4 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion is about 10 meters with a height of 2.5 meters. The tumulus is orientated to the south, and the west side is slightly deformed. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' or shards of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' have been found, nor has any trace of a moat been discovered. The horizontal-entry cave-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a multi-chambered structure in the center of the posterior circle, opening to the west. The back room is 3.7 meters long, about 2.2 meters wide, and about 3.6 meters high, with a stone house shaped {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus}} attached to the rear wall, and the front room is about half the size of the back room. It was already opened in 1673, and no {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} have been passed down. The tumulus is a [[decorated kofun]]. The pillar stones and back wall are divided into sections with a triangular pattern made of vertical, horizontal, and diagonal line carvings, and each section is painted with red, yellow, and blue pigments, and the upper part is painted red. Judging from the structure of the burial chamber and the shape of the tumulus, it is believed to have been constructed in the 6th century, or the last Kofun period. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> From the five small {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular tumuli surrounding the main tumulus, iron arrowheads, metal utensils (bow fittings), and shards of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} pottery have been found.
Although many decorated ''kofun'' have been found along the coast of the {{ill|Ariake Sea|en|Ariake Sea|WD=Q49557|lt=Ariake Sea}} in the Kyushu, this is the only one to have been found in along the coast of the Genkai Sea, and its presence outside the distribution area for decorative ''kofun'' suggests cultural or political interaction between the regions. This ''kofun'' was included in the {{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|WD=Q7809|lt=UNESCO}} {{ill|World Heritage Site|en|World Heritage Site|WD=Q9259|lt=World Heritage Site}} {{ill|Sacred Island of Okinoshima and Associated Sites in the Munakata Region|en|Sacred Island of Okinoshima and Associated Sites in the Munakata Region|WD=Q324638|lt=Sacred Island of Okinoshima and Associated Sites in the Munakata Region}} submission in 2009, but was later removed from the constituent assets when it was officially registered in 2017.<ref name="Okinoshima">{{cite web |url=https://whc.unesco.org/en/tentativelists/5400/ |title=Okinoshima Island and Related Sites in Munakata Region |publisher={{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|WD=Q7809|lt=UNESCO}} |access-date=7 December 2013}}</ref><ref name="Okinoshima2">{{cite web |url=http://www.okinoshima-heritage.jp/pamphlets/img/en/pdf/OkinoshimaIsland.pdf |title=Okinoshima Island and Related Sites in Munakata Region |publisher=World Heritage Promotion Committee of "Okinoshima Island and Related Sites in Munakata Region" |pages=6f |access-date=7 December 2013 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131212072806/http://www.okinoshima-heritage.jp/pamphlets/img/en/pdf/OkinoshimaIsland.pdf |archive-date=12 December 2013 }}</ref>
The tumulus is approximately 2.4 kilometers northwest of [[:en:Munakata Shrine|Munakata Shrine]] or 7.4 kilometers (15 minutes by car]] from {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|en|Kagoshima Main Line|WD=Q1187520|lt=Kagoshima Main Line}} {{ill|Tōgō Station|en|Tōgō Station|WD=Q5359652|lt=Tōgō Station}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|WD=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}} *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.munakata.lg.jp/w018/030/020/020/500/201501270052.html Munakata City home page] {{in lang|ja}} Sakurakyō Kofun

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Munakata, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Sakurakyō Kofun | native_name =桜京古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sakurakyo Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sakurakyō Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sakurakyō Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.843797|N|130.495467|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Munakata, Fukuoka|en|Munakata, Fukuoka|WD=Q819603|lt=Munakata, Fukuoka|1=Munakata, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|50|38|N|130|29|43|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sakurakyō Kofun'''|桜京古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Mutajiri neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Munakata, Fukuoka|en|Munakata, Fukuoka|WD=Q819603|lt=Munakata|1=Munakata, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/139747|title=桜京古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sakurakyō Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is the center of a cluster of approximately 80 Kofuns facing the [[Genkai Sea]] on a hill with an elevation of about 42 meters. It has a total length is about 41 meters; the diameter of the posterior circular portion is about 18 meters, the height is about 4 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion is about 10 meters with a height of 2.5 meters. The tumulus is orientated to the south, and the west side is slightly deformed. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' or shards of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found, nor has any trace of a moat been discovered. The horizontal-entry cave-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a multi-chambered structure in the center of the posterior circle, opening to the west. The back room is 3.7 meters long, about 2.2 meters wide, and about 3.6 meters high, with a stone house shaped {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} attached to the rear wall, and the front room is about half the size of the back room. It was already opened in 1673, and no {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} have been passed down. The tumulus is a [[decorated kofun]]. The pillar stones and back wall are divided into sections with a triangular pattern made of vertical, horizontal, and diagonal line carvings, and each section is painted with red, yellow, and blue pigments, and the upper part is painted red. Judging from the structure of the burial chamber and the shape of the tumulus, it is believed to have been constructed in the 6th century, or the last Kofun period. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> From the five small {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular tumuli surrounding the main tumulus, iron arrowheads, metal utensils (bow fittings), and shards of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} pottery have been found.
Although many decorated ''kofun'' have been found along the coast of the {{ill|Ariake Sea|en|Ariake Sea|WD=Q49557|lt=Ariake Sea|1=Ariake Sea}} in the Kyushu, this is the only one to have been found in along the coast of the Genkai Sea, and its presence outside the distribution area for decorative ''kofun'' suggests cultural or political interaction between the regions. This ''kofun'' was included in the {{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|WD=Q7809|lt=UNESCO|1=UNESCO}} {{ill|World Heritage Site|en|World Heritage Site|WD=Q9259|lt=World Heritage Site|1=World Heritage Site}} {{ill|Sacred Island of Okinoshima and Associated Sites in the Munakata Region|en|Sacred Island of Okinoshima and Associated Sites in the Munakata Region|WD=Q324638|lt=Sacred Island of Okinoshima and Associated Sites in the Munakata Region|1=Sacred Island of Okinoshima and Associated Sites in the Munakata Region}} submission in 2009, but was later removed from the constituent assets when it was officially registered in 2017.<ref name="Okinoshima">{{cite web |url=https://whc.unesco.org/en/tentativelists/5400/ |title=Okinoshima Island and Related Sites in Munakata Region |publisher={{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|WD=Q7809|lt=UNESCO|1=UNESCO}} |access-date=7 December 2013}}</ref><ref name="Okinoshima2">{{cite web |url=http://www.okinoshima-heritage.jp/pamphlets/img/en/pdf/OkinoshimaIsland.pdf |title=Okinoshima Island and Related Sites in Munakata Region |publisher=World Heritage Promotion Committee of "Okinoshima Island and Related Sites in Munakata Region" |pages=6f |access-date=7 December 2013 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131212072806/http://www.okinoshima-heritage.jp/pamphlets/img/en/pdf/OkinoshimaIsland.pdf |archive-date=12 December 2013 }}</ref>
The tumulus is approximately 2.4 kilometers northwest of [[:en:Munakata Shrine|Munakata Shrine]] or 7.4 kilometers (15 minutes by car]] from {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|en|Kagoshima Main Line|WD=Q1187520|lt=Kagoshima Main Line|1=Kagoshima Main Line}} {{ill|Tōgō Station|en|Tōgō Station|WD=Q5359652|lt=Tōgō Station|1=Tōgō Station}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|WD=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}} *[[Decorated kofun]]

This ''kofun'' was included in the {{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|WD=Q7809|lt=UNESCO|1=UNESCO}} {{ill|World Heritage Site|en|World Heritage Site|WD=Q9259|lt=World Heritage Site|1=World Heritage Site}} {{ill|Sacred Island of Okinoshima and Associated Sites in the Munakata Region|en|Sacred Island of Okinoshima and Associated Sites in the Munakata Region|WD=Q324638|lt=Sacred Island of Okinoshima and Associated Sites in the Munakata Region|1=Sacred Island of Okinoshima and Associated Sites in the Munakata Region}} submission in 2009, but was later removed from the constituent assets when it was officially registered in 2017.<ref name="Okinoshima">{{cite web |url=https://whc.unesco.org/en/tentativelists/5400/ |title=Okinoshima Island and Related Sites in Munakata Region |publisher={{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|WD=Q7809|lt=UNESCO|1=UNESCO}} |access-date=7 December 2013}}</ref><ref name="Okinoshima2">{{cite web |url=http://www.okinoshima-heritage.jp/pamphlets/img/en/pdf/OkinoshimaIsland.pdf |title=Okinoshima Island and Related Sites in Munakata Region |publisher=World Heritage Promotion Committee of "Okinoshima Island and Related Sites in Munakata Region" |pages=6f |access-date=7 December 2013 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131212072806/http://www.okinoshima-heritage.jp/pamphlets/img/en/pdf/OkinoshimaIsland.pdf |archive-date=12 December 2013 }}</ref>
The tumulus is approximately 2.4 kilometers northwest of [[:en:Munakata Shrine|Munakata Shrine]] or 7.4 kilometers (15 minutes by car]] from {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|en|Kagoshima Main Line|WD=Q1187520|lt=Kagoshima Main Line|1=Kagoshima Main Line}} {{ill|Tōgō Station|en|Tōgō Station|WD=Q5359652|lt=Tōgō Station|1=Tōgō Station}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|WD=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}} *[[Decorated kofun]]

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Munakata, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Sakurakyō Kofun | native_name =桜京古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sakurakyo Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sakurakyō Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sakurakyō Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.843797|N|130.495467|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Munakata, Fukuoka|en|Munakata, Fukuoka|WD=Q819603|lt=Munakata, Fukuoka|1=Munakata, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|50|38|N|130|29|43|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sakurakyō Kofun'''|桜京古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Mutajiri neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Munakata, Fukuoka|en|Munakata, Fukuoka|WD=Q819603|lt=Munakata|1=Munakata, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/139747|title=桜京古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sakurakyō Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is the center of a cluster of approximately 80 Kofuns facing the [[Genkai Sea]] on a hill with an elevation of about 42 meters. It has a total length is about 41 meters; the diameter of the posterior circular portion is about 18 meters, the height is about 4 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion is about 10 meters with a height of 2.5 meters. The tumulus is orientated to the south, and the west side is slightly deformed. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' or shards of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found, nor has any trace of a moat been discovered. The horizontal-entry cave-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a multi-chambered structure in the center of the posterior circle, opening to the west. The back room is 3.7 meters long, about 2.2 meters wide, and about 3.6 meters high, with a stone house shaped {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} attached to the rear wall, and the front room is about half the size of the back room. It was already opened in 1673, and no {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} have been passed down. The tumulus is a [[decorated kofun]]. The pillar stones and back wall are divided into sections with a triangular pattern made of vertical, horizontal, and diagonal line carvings, and each section is painted with red, yellow, and blue pigments, and the upper part is painted red. Judging from the structure of the burial chamber and the shape of the tumulus, it is believed to have been constructed in the 6th century, or the last Kofun period. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> From the five small {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular tumuli surrounding the main tumulus, iron arrowheads, metal utensils (bow fittings), and shards of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} pottery have been found.
Although many decorated ''kofun'' have been found along the coast of the {{ill|Ariake Sea|en|Ariake Sea|WD=Q49557|lt=Ariake Sea|1=Ariake Sea}} in the Kyushu, this is the only one to have been found in along the coast of the Genkai Sea, and its presence outside the distribution area for decorative ''kofun'' suggests cultural or political interaction between the regions. This ''kofun'' was included in the {{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|WD=Q7809|lt=UNESCO|1=UNESCO}} {{ill|World Heritage Site|en|World Heritage Site|WD=Q9259|lt=World Heritage Site|1=World Heritage Site}} {{ill|Sacred Island of Okinoshima and Associated Sites in the Munakata Region|en|Sacred Island of Okinoshima and Associated Sites in the Munakata Region|WD=Q324638|lt=Sacred Island of Okinoshima and Associated Sites in the Munakata Region|1=Sacred Island of Okinoshima and Associated Sites in the Munakata Region}} submission in 2009, but was later removed from the constituent assets when it was officially registered in 2017.<ref name="Okinoshima">{{cite web |url=https://whc.unesco.org/en/tentativelists/5400/ |title=Okinoshima Island and Related Sites in Munakata Region |publisher={{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|WD=Q7809|lt=UNESCO|1=UNESCO}} |access-date=7 December 2013}}</ref><ref name="Okinoshima2">{{cite web |url=http://www.okinoshima-heritage.jp/pamphlets/img/en/pdf/OkinoshimaIsland.pdf |title=Okinoshima Island and Related Sites in Munakata Region |publisher=World Heritage Promotion Committee of "Okinoshima Island and Related Sites in Munakata Region" |pages=6f |access-date=7 December 2013 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131212072806/http://www.okinoshima-heritage.jp/pamphlets/img/en/pdf/OkinoshimaIsland.pdf |archive-date=12 December 2013 }}</ref>
The tumulus is approximately 2.4 kilometers northwest of [[:en:Munakata Shrine|Munakata Shrine]] or 7.4 kilometers (15 minutes by car]] from {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|en|Kagoshima Main Line|WD=Q1187520|lt=Kagoshima Main Line|1=Kagoshima Main Line}} {{ill|Tōgō Station|en|Tōgō Station|WD=Q5359652|lt=Tōgō Station|1=Tōgō Station}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|WD=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}} *[[Decorated kofun]]

The {{nihongo|'''Sakurakyō Kofun'''|桜京古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Mutajiri neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Munakata, Fukuoka|en|Munakata, Fukuoka|WD=Q819603|lt=Munakata|1=Munakata, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/139747|title=桜京古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Sakurakyō Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is the center of a cluster of approximately 80 Kofuns facing the [[Genkai Sea]] on a hill with an elevation of about 42 meters. It has a total length is about 41 meters; the diameter of the posterior circular portion is about 18 meters, the height is about 4 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion is about 10 meters with a height of 2.5 meters. The tumulus is orientated to the south, and the west side is slightly deformed. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' or shards of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found, nor has any trace of a moat been discovered. The horizontal-entry cave-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a multi-chambered structure in the center of the posterior circle, opening to the west. The back room is 3.7 meters long, about 2.2 meters wide, and about 3.6 meters high, with a stone house shaped {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} attached to the rear wall, and the front room is about half the size of the back room. It was already opened in 1673, and no {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} have been passed down. The tumulus is a [[decorated kofun]]. The pillar stones and back wall are divided into sections with a triangular pattern made of vertical, horizontal, and diagonal line carvings, and each section is painted with red, yellow, and blue pigments, and the upper part is painted red. Judging from the structure of the burial chamber and the shape of the tumulus, it is believed to have been constructed in the 6th century, or the last Kofun period. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> From the five small {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular tumuli surrounding the main tumulus, iron arrowheads, metal utensils (bow fittings), and shards of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} pottery have been found.
Although many decorated ''kofun'' have been found along the coast of the {{ill|Ariake Sea|en|Ariake Sea|WD=Q49557|lt=Ariake Sea|1=Ariake Sea}} in the Kyushu, this is the only one to have been found in along the coast of the Genkai Sea, and its presence outside the distribution area for decorative ''kofun'' suggests cultural or political interaction between the regions. This ''kofun'' was included in the {{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|WD=Q7809|lt=UNESCO|1=UNESCO}} {{ill|World Heritage Site|en|World Heritage Site|WD=Q9259|lt=World Heritage Site|1=World Heritage Site}} {{ill|Sacred Island of Okinoshima and Associated Sites in the Munakata Region|en|Sacred Island of Okinoshima and Associated Sites in the Munakata Region|WD=Q324638|lt=Sacred Island of Okinoshima and Associated Sites in the Munakata Region|1=Sacred Island of Okinoshima and Associated Sites in the Munakata Region}} submission in 2009, but was later removed from the constituent assets when it was officially registered in 2017.<ref name="Okinoshima">{{cite web |url=https://whc.unesco.org/en/tentativelists/5400/ |title=Okinoshima Island and Related Sites in Munakata Region |publisher={{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|WD=Q7809|lt=UNESCO|1=UNESCO}} |access-date=7 December 2013}}</ref><ref name="Okinoshima2">{{cite web |url=http://www.okinoshima-heritage.jp/pamphlets/img/en/pdf/OkinoshimaIsland.pdf |title=Okinoshima Island and Related Sites in Munakata Region |publisher=World Heritage Promotion Committee of "Okinoshima Island and Related Sites in Munakata Region" |pages=6f |access-date=7 December 2013 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131212072806/http://www.okinoshima-heritage.jp/pamphlets/img/en/pdf/OkinoshimaIsland.pdf |archive-date=12 December 2013 }}</ref>
The tumulus is approximately 2.4 kilometers northwest of [[:en:Munakata Shrine|Munakata Shrine]] or 7.4 kilometers (15 minutes by car]] from {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|en|Kagoshima Main Line|WD=Q1187520|lt=Kagoshima Main Line|1=Kagoshima Main Line}} {{ill|Tōgō Station|en|Tōgō Station|WD=Q5359652|lt=Tōgō Station|1=Tōgō Station}}.
